---
title: "Rittenhouse and Redd Tree Inventory for Newark Parks and Recreation (July 2026)"
subtitle: "An Inventory of Tree Resources in Rittenhouse and Redd Parks"
author:
  - name: Matt
affiliation:
  - name: Technology Service Center, University of Delaware
    url: https://www.sites.udel.edu/technology-service-center
date: July 6, 2026
version: "1.0"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  echo: false
---

**Prepared by**  
Technology Service Center, University of Delaware  

**Prepared for**  
City of Newark Parks & Recreation  
Newark, Delaware

## Contact Information
For questions regarding this report, please contact the Technology Service Center at University of Delaware.

## Motivation

The purpose of this inventory is to identify, measure, and document significant trees across Rittenhouse Park and Redd Park. This data collection effort provides a comprehensive baseline understanding of the tree resources in these parks.

## Overview

This inventory documents trees surveyed in Rittenhouse Park (RH-P), capturing essential metrics including tree height, diameter at breast height (DBH), canopy spread, and overall condition. The collected data supports park management decisions and long-term planning for urban forestry.

## Inventoried Locations

Data was collected from Rittenhouse Park and Redd Park, with a focus on documenting significant tree species and their characteristics.
